purchase_triggers.context: Replacing chair wheels, buying new shredder

objections.objection: Product breaks or fails after short use
Poor value due to high cost and quick depletion (ink cartridges)
Design is functional but unattractive
Product is adjustable but doesn't function correctly
Product is too plain or depressing without customization

switching_triggers.reason: Chair broke after only 3 months
Ink flow stops easily, runs out quickly, or is scratchy
Does not securely hold contents

deal_breakers: Core function fails (e.g., pen won't write, chair breaks)

pain_points.signal: Difficulty finding preferred products in local stores due to supply issues
Products looking cheap, plastic, or low quality
Product features being misleading or incomplete (e.g., printer cartridges missing required components)
Pens bleeding through paper
Products being too shiny or not matte when desired
Assembling furniture/products requiring tools and time
Concern about items rolling off shelves with no lip
Packaging materials not being suitable for heavy-duty shipping
Necklace-style reader holders causing dangling glasses or bib-like effect

desired_outcomes.outcome: Easy sliding/access to items on shelves (no lip)
Strong, secure magnets for glasses holders
Products that work exactly as hoped/described
Convenient, safe home delivery (avoiding in-store shopping)
Products that save time (e.g., pre-strung tags)

purchase_triggers.trigger: Need for organization and storage (moving, packing, kitchen appliances)
Replacing unsatisfactory older versions of products (reader holders, pens)
purchase_triggers.context: Product improvement, upgrading from past failures

purchase_triggers.trigger: Gifting or holiday preparations (cards, wrap, bubble wrap)
Supporting elderly family members (calendar, cushion)
Teaching tools for children (sand timer for brushing)
Backup insurance for essential items (magnet replacements)

objections.objection: Product looking cheap or low-quality
Misleading compatibility information
Unacceptable performance (bleeding through paper)
Excessive shininess vs desired matte finish
Incomplete product sets missing necessary components

switching_triggers.reason: Dislike of string around neck and dangling glasses
switching_triggers.threshold: Stronger magnets and better placement available

switching_triggers.reason: Consistent disappointment with performance
Insufficient strength or functionality

pain_points.signal: Inconsistent ink flow (skipping, stopping, drying out)
Poor build quality (defective, damaged, missing parts)
Damaged products upon arrival (dents, bends, tears, dirt)
Disappointing paper quality (too thin, see-through, uneven texture)
Difficult returns process (costs, scheduling, drop-offs)
Strong chemical odor causing allergic reactions
Leaking pens (ink on fingers, messy)

desired_outcomes.outcome: Consistent ink flow across pens/inks (no skipping, smooth writing)
Durable products that survive shipping and daily use
Fountain pen-friendly paper (no feathering, handles ink well)
Easy returns for defective/damaged items (free pickup, accessible)
Lightweight pens for reduced hand strain during long writing

purchase_triggers.trigger: Positive reviews from other users
Beautiful color options
Need for specialized paper for fountain pens
Company donates a product for each purchase
Personalization/customization potential
Replacement for worn-out/broken items
Collecting pens in favorite colors

objections.objection: Price too high for perceived value (overpriced plastic)
Unreliable quality control (bad batches, manufacturing flaws)
Packaging fails to protect products during shipping
Poor seller/supplier communication
Health/safety concerns (allergens, chemical smells, cancer warnings)
Incorrect items shipped (wrong colors, wrong products)
Design features causing discomfort (scratchy nibs, uncomfortable grips)

switching_triggers.from_product: Specific ink brand (e.g., blue ink from brand)
switching_triggers.reason: Extremely dry, keeps drying in pen
switching_triggers.threshold: Multiple refill attempts with no improvement

switching_triggers.reason: Skipping, inconsistent flow, scratchy nibs
Too heavy to lift due to disability

deal_breakers: Defective items that don't work at all
Unacceptable return costs for damaged goods
False advertising (product differs significantly from images)

pain_points.signal: Electronic components failing immediately upon first use
Difficulty accessing battery compartments requiring special tools (tiny screwdriver)
Product not fitting the intended use (stamp too large for small envelopes)
Items arriving disorganized/open, spilling contents in packaging
Product material is too thin or flimsy (mousepad, paper)
Adhesive stickers not sticking on certain surfaces (oily/greasy)
Stickers being too small to write useful information
Receiving a fundamentally defective or low-quality primary product (printer)

desired_outcomes.outcome: Accurately count and track savings (coins) without manual effort
Aid a child's learning/development with easier-to-use tools (grip, writing)
Efficiently organize and price items for a garage sale
Permanently label or mark items for easy identification
Organize physical items and information (business cards, recipes, tools)
Easily move heavy or bulky items without strain
Have reliable, high-quality writing instruments for professional use
Achieve sentimental value or humor through product design

purchase_triggers.trigger: Need to replace a tedious manual process (counting coins manually)
Preparing for a specific, recurring event (neighborhood garage sale)
Supporting a child's educational activity
Needing a specific tool/supply immediately for a remote job or project
Replenishing a consumable that is frequently used/gifted
Organizing after a major life event (moving into a new house)
Seeking a more convenient version of a common tool (retractable vs. capped pen)

objections.objection: Initial product failure requiring troubleshooting/reset
Poor design making the product awkward to use (jar too heavy for side handles, coin opening too small)
Product finish/quality differs from expectation (shiny vs. matte, thin paper)
Packaging fails, causing product disarray upon delivery
Product size is impractical for the intended task (stamp too large, stickers too small)
Product is perceived as lightweight/low-durability for its function

switching_triggers.reason: Banks no longer accept rolled coins; desire for automated counting
switching_triggers.threshold: Process becomes too tedious or unsupported by institutions

switching_triggers.reason: Fear of data loss/crashes; desire for physical reliability
switching_triggers.threshold: Concern over digital reliability outweighs convenience

switching_triggers.reason: Desire for one-handed, convenient operation
switching_triggers.threshold: Frustration with losing caps or two-handed operation

switching_triggers.reason: Need more writing space for pricing/info

deal_breakers: Complete product failure upon first use with no fix
Design flaw preventing core functionality (e.g., can't access contents)
Product arrives damaged or unusable due to poor packaging
Critical component is fundamentally defective (e.g., printer is 'total junk')

top_pain_points: Inconsistent ink flow (skipping, stopping, drying out)
Poor build quality (defective, damaged, missing parts)
Damaged products upon arrival (dents, bends, tears, dirt)
Disappointing paper quality (too thin, see-through, uneven texture)
Difficult returns process (costs, scheduling, drop-offs)
Strong chemical odor causing allergic reactions
Leaking pens (ink on fingers, messy)
Previous product falling apart or not durable
Tools (sharpeners) that waste material (eats pencils)
Pockets overflowing/needing to carry many items
top_desired_outcomes: Consistent ink flow across pens/inks (no skipping, smooth writing)
Durable products that survive shipping and daily use
Fountain pen-friendly paper (no feathering, handles ink well)
Easy returns for defective/damaged items (free pickup, accessible)
Lightweight pens for reduced hand strain during long writing
Precise, fine-point sharpening for detail work
top_deal_breakers: Defective items that don't work at all
Unacceptable return costs for damaged goods
False advertising (product differs significantly from images)
Critical design flaw making product unusable
